sentences.text: long.
That was a good one.
Reset?
Okay.
Yeah, that was funny.
Made a good joke.
Thanks, Martin.
Hello?
Hey, how are y'all?
Good, how are you?
June.
Typical… what is it today?
Wednesday?
Exactly, that's what's typical.
I think they all feel the same except for Friday.
Yeah.
Look, even my Fridays are bleeding into the rest of the week.
It's just… it's just 5 straight Mondays lately.
So….
So, you know, I mean, not sure if, Not sure if any of your current clients have Made the whispers, but there's whispers.
And it's making my life.
Interesting, so….
What are the whispers?
I was like….
Okay, so it hasn't… it hasn't hit y'all.
Mean me?
I don't know.
If you're gonna talk about the big acquisition, then yes, we have her.
That, okay.
Okay, yeah.
Those are… those are shouts for the mountaintops.
at this point, they might as well be.
I'm even… you know, we've only… I've known, just….
Yeah.
for a good minute.
It was made public last week to the rest of the company.
I've known for a little while, though.
And, yeah, those clients are already coming to myself and to Alex and saying, you know, hey.
we don't know if what FPI's telling us is the same thing that assets… now they're trying to compare and measure… measure up against what FPI was doing, so….
-Oh.
Yeah, I bet.
That's a huge piece of news, though.
But yeah, we did… it did… the whisper made it to us.
That's all I've been getting for the last, like, 3 months is, Brandon, is it true?
Is it true?
And I'm like, I'm not saying anything until Ryan says it.
Smart.
And that's a huge deal, like, that's probably one… that has to be the biggest takeover slash acquisition in the industry.
That works.
I think EDR with Graystar was technically larger when Graystar picked up EDR back.
Years and years ago.
maybe… I… if anything was bigger than this, it would have been Graystar picking up a platform.
That's basically the only… the only one that's bigger.
Yeah, I was thinking maybe Wood Partners.
Wood Partners is only, like, 20, 20,000 units.
I think FPI is, like….
Yeah, huge, and I don't think EDR was that big.
Not by door count.
I think EDR was pretty… I mean, it definitely wasn't 6 figures, if I remember.
Even… Well, that's okay.
Has it been, … announced online?
I feel like I haven't seen a ton of it online.
It just kind of just whispers and talks to the industry.
So, we are likely not gonna put out a full public statement about that.
Okay.
I think it's probably just gonna be an organic owner-to-owner thing.
Okay.

Hey, we just realized, you know, FPI, our VPs told us, asset's now managing the properties, they are now under asset, and owner-to-owner is gonna start trickling it out to other people in the industry.
But I mean.
you're talking number 2 just acquired number 6 on the top 50 list.
I don't know that we need a press release for it.
So… And we'll… maybe this hasn't been decided yet, Brandon, but will, FPI continue to be, like, called FBI, or will that become… assets.
They… yeah, the… I've heard a couple of different ways.
The first way is it's gonna do exactly what Lund did.
When we picked up Lund, they ceased to be Lund companies, they're now all asset living.
Okay.
everything got rebranded.
To my knowledge, I think FBI's gonna end up.
rebranding completely to Asset Living.
Some of their email signatures have already been updated.
Yeah.

You know, there's… talks and whispers in the company that maybe it'd be called, FPI by Asset Living, or, you know, even… or something, something like that, but I have a feeling it's just gonna be a full merger.
Yeah.
That's it.
Because what happened?
Is First Communities still called First Communities, or has that kind of slowly transformed over to asset livings?
I know it was First Communities for a while.
Yeah, it stayed first communities for a while, it's now all asset-less.
Okay.
Funny.
that natural timeline will… it'll be FPI for a little bit, and then everything will just kind of become asset.
Yeah, the only company we bought that really just hasn't wanted to change has been Strategic Management Partners.
They don't call themselves SMP anymore, but they still keep it in all of their logos, they have all their email signatures, their properties, try to… you know, still use it sometimes.
Even their… their… EVPs still say, yeah, SMP, oops, asset.
So….
And how long have you been an asset?
… 3 years next month.
Nice.
Okay, so you see.
No.
Yeah, I came in right after, …
right after they picked up JMG, or right before… Yeah, right before the contract was inked for JMG.
Were you at JMG?
No, I was with a small a foreclosure management company called Tarantino Properties.
They did some owner-operator stuff, and they managed a bunch of foreclosures.
I was, …
I have community manager slash quality manager, quality control for a construction project on student housing.
I'm familiar with Tarantino.
… Texas, right?
Yeah, yeah, I'm based out of Houston, Sal Thomas, Anthony.
Zach, Zach Tactics is probably the person that would have reached me.
He's very much into being tech-forward and marketing forward.
He would have been the one, if anybody, that y'all would have had.
I think we have Christy on this call.
Do you know if anybody else is joining, or should we….
I… I mean, if… I don't… I don't see Kirsty or Kelsey.
Yeah.
Christy an email a minute or two ago, just to see if she was still joining, because I know she wanted to be on, but…
Nothing back yet.
I will tease them and see if they want to jump on.

If we have to push a time to when everybody can join, or if you think it's helpful to just have you here, usually we're doing a kickoff just to recap where we're at with implementation, the properties are… Haven't chosen and what to expect going forward.
Yeah, I mean, so, I don't know how much Kirsty's really explained.
Kirstie and I basically set up what was the initial marketing… initial marketing technology department.
It was never called anything.
We were just people.
And we exist… hey, there's Christy!
We were people, and we existed as marketing team members, but we set up this, the origins of marketing systems and assets.
So that's what my team does now, is strictly all the implementation.
If someone wants a technology that we don't offer, we don't have an MSA with, I handle the pilot success measurements and pilot success goals.
And all the things that come with that, such as debugging and, hey, Brandon, this did a weird thing, we don't know why.
So that's kind of the background here.
So I'll be helping and doing a lot of the implementation on Maven, like I've done with several of the new dev properties with Noah and Serena and Sam, like, anything like that.
I know they usually give you guys access to, like, socials, I'll jump in and do GMBs, obviously I'll do PMS credentials, … Try and turn these… this… this pilot on pretty quickly for you guys.

Yeah, I've seen your name come through a lot on those, and I always know how fast you are, because it's like, I swear within minutes of you getting an email, there's a response that it's been done already, so it's always much appreciated, and I love seeing your name pop up.

Yeah, that's the… that's why Kirsty and I… we… I mean, almost 3 years ago now, that's why we… we recognized this was a need, and Christy's honed her… her craft in other ways, so she's still very, very fluent in the marketing systems, but I jumped in and, really
I don't know, just… learned things that I probably shouldn't have learned at the time, but I know them now, so it's great.
I love that.
Hi, Christy.
Hey, Kelsey.
Oh my god.
Bye!
It's been one of these days where, like, those calls just keep coming in, and they're coming in unscheduled.
Like, the last one, I even said, I only have 9 minutes, and then it happened, and I'm sorry.
That's okay.
That's okay.
I was trying to get a report that's needed out that's been, like, consuming my last couple of days, and I all of a sudden saw Brandon's message and was like, oh, so sorry!
Okay.
Totally fine.
It's one of those weeks.
Yes, it is.
One of those weeks for, like, 3 years now, so….
That'd be awesome.
We were just talking about the, huge news with you guys.
expanding, so I imagine it's not gonna get any less hectic or exciting around there.
It's really not, so just send help, send prayers, figure out how to clone.
out.
Whatever you need to do, I'm… I'm good with.
Send coffee.
Clone U3, yeah, send coffee.
Yes, Kathy?
Caffeine, always appreciated.
I've tried to go off coffee recently for 2 weeks, and I thought I was gonna die, so I gave that up.
Thank you.
Not worth it.
I'm not even gonna attempt it.
Me either, I'd rather die, honestly.
That sounds bad, but, like, I need it.
It's the reason I wake up in the morning most of the time.
I'm like, let's go get my coffee.

Well, we don't want to take too much more of your time, so we can hop into it, but we wanted to, take the time to go over what else is needed for implementation, and then also just kind of get on the same page for what the partnership will look like in general, and how these typically roll out.

So… Before I hop into that, I did want to ask, I know you had sent over… Christy, you had started with the two properties, and then the recent list was 61 properties, so I just wanted to ask how those 61 properties were chosen for this.

So, instead of just rolling this out to the entirety of the Phoenix Division, I'm a huge believer in making sure that after a pilot's been initiated, knowing how much goes into the back end of implementing.
that the teams and the properties will actually be able to afford the product moving forward.
I'd never want to do something that seems, like, somewhat in vain and just because, so I rolled this out and asked the regionals to tell me where they thought that this would be most beneficial.
So instead of doing just a full-blown way like, wave of setups.

We wanted to have things that would be a little more intentional for properties that just truly can't afford to have an SEO backing, the teams are just not savvy enough to really be on the Google listings, you know, the integrations between the systems, all the things, just the ones that really need that leg up.
So, that's what you're seeing, and then some of them are my, you know, little children that I'm like, of course I want you to have this.
So, that's kind of the mix.
Of what you're seeing there.
Yeah, okay.
Yeah, makes sense.
And are these properties, are they all in similar regions?
I know you said it's multiple regionals, but are they.
Yeah.
States, or…?
It's… it's multiple states, so what you have there is going to be… there are some Texas, there are some New Mexico, there's Colorado, and Nevada, and Arizona.
So it's… it's a… it's a wide range, but it's mostly all what would be considered Southwest.
Okay, yeah, nice.
So we're getting a mix of different states in here, too, which is nice.
Yes, absolutely.
Cool.
Well, we are thinking, agenda-wise for the call today, introduce ourselves.
So, we've talked a lot, but we've never, gotten on and done a meeting altogether.
So… wait, can you guys see this?
No.
No.
Oh, okay.
I was like, your faces just went away, that's why.
There we go.
Haven't done that in a while.
So, go through introductions so you know who you'll be working with, and then align on the outcomes of this.
So, how are you guys measuring success of this product, and what will a successful pilot look like for you guys?
And then go over implementation process, timeline on that.
And then what to expect post-kickoff.
So, once all these properties are up and running and live on the product, what to expect after that.
And then there is a document in here for best practices.
So, what we've learned over time through rolling this out with other customers, the type of content that Google likes, what type of photos Google likes, that's in here as well.
Anything else that you guys wanted to add into this?
Not me.
I don't think so.
No, I'm good.
Alright.
Sorry, Patrick, you're not on this slide again.
I was gonna say, it's two meetings in a row I got cut from the Meet the Team slide, so it's okay.
I'm doing okay.
You can't sit with us!
Well, you can verbally introduce yourself.
We'll get your photo on here next time.
I'm Kylie.
I've been talking with probably all of you in different times for different teams and different rollouts, but I've been working with the asset team overall for the last about 2 years.
So, mostly working with Shelby on the new dev team, Rosalie for the student team, and then we've been recently talking with other parts of the company as well.
So, excited to be working with you guys and starting the partnership with Phoenix.
I am Morgan, nice to meet you guys.
I'm on the customer success team here and managing a lot of our larger enterprise accounts, with Kylie as well.

I've also been overseeing the Asset Living account for a few years, been with the company for about 5, so pretty familiar with the same points of contact that Kylie mentioned, and kind of how your whole portfolio is divided.
So I'm your go-to person.
for checking calls, reporting, any really day-to-day needs that you have, but Kylie will also still be supporting the account with me as we go forward.

My name's Patrick, I've been at the company almost 9 years, lead the sales team, work closely with Kylie and Morgan have met a bunch of your team members, and we love working with the asset crew, so excited to be getting this, next stage and this pilot off the ground.
Do you need us to introduce ourselves, or do you get it?
If you want to… I don't want to skip over you, but….
I'm Christy Powers, you guys have been working with me, I'm the Senior Marketing Manager over in Phoenix.
I've got a portfolio of about 25, 26, and then do… try to assist as much as I can for the remainder of that portfolio of all of Phoenix.
I'll go next.
I'm Kelsey Foster, I'm a marketing associate in Phoenix, and I'm newer to the team and just was assigned my portfolio of about, 14.
Awesome.
Nice to meet you guys.
Brandon, we just chatted for quite a bit, so….
Yeah, we've had a few emails, one or two.
Okay.
Cool.
Jumping in, just a quick contract overview.

Obviously, this is new, it was just signed, but just to reiterate, you had signed on 61 properties for this contract, plus your two, Christy, for both the Google Business Automation Tool, and then the social automation tool as well.
Term length, we're getting this kicked off right now, but we pushed the billing start date to 1-1, so it's an annual agreement through the end of next year.
Any questions on that?

wanted to put this slide in here, because there's different conversations that I had with different people, they were different calls, so the… what we heard overall from the different… conversations that I had, with you guys on the call, and then Lindsey as well, was that there was a need to expand the features from the current, or I guess, previous Google Business process.
And then we know that some of the properties were manually creating, posting, scheduling content.
Obviously, that is a time-consuming process, and what I heard was automation would be helpful for giving the team some time back, and consistency across profiles.

And then as far as the features that I heard that were most exciting to you guys, it was the pricing and availability feed to… directly from your feed to your Google profiles, along with concessions and the automated Q&A.
And then on the social side, I heard that automation for social posts would also give the team some time back, and then increase consistency, profile to profile.
Is there anything that I'm missing there?
Nope, and I already saw that we had everything set up for Elysion at Rainbow, and, like, that listing looks so cool now.
Yeah, yeah.
Nice.
Yeah, I love that.
Jumping into onboarding timeline, so we are doing the kickoff call right now.
That's to meet us and then align on goals, timeline, further communication.
And then over the next few weeks after implementation's done, the… the goal is to schedule ongoing check-ins.
So, we typically recommend in the beginning of kicking off a new partnership like this to meet every month.
Does that work for you guys?
We obviously want to be aware of your time and know that you guys are busy with a lot right now, but what are your thoughts on, like, a quick check-in call every month?
I'm gonna tell you that that sounds really doable.
Okay.
Sounds.
Yeah, and just like today's call, obviously, we can put it on the calendar and then shift it around as needed.

We understand you guys have a lot going on right now, so we could always pencil it in and then move it around as needed, but the goal for those calls is to, obviously, review the profiles, so go over what you're seeing live on the profiles, but then also connect on new features and additional ways we can optimize the profiles.
And then, obviously, always getting feedback from you guys.
Sounds good.
Alright, jumping into implementation.
So, these are the two steps, that we need on your end to get everything fully live.
So that would be granting… you've done this many times, Brandon, but granting the Google management access, and then turning the feed syndication on.
Those are the two steps.
Any questions on any of those, or do you guys need anything from us on those two?

… No, I can get the PMS stuff done, 60 sites is gonna take… a couple days with the programs team, because they are at Intrada, Google Access, that's probably one day between the three of us, honestly.
Okay.
And social profiles, we'll have to engage in another team, so that's probably 2 or 3 days in total, depending on their availability, so… start tomorrow, everything should be active by Monday evening.
Okay.
And Brandon, we're gonna send in separate tickets based on PMS with the property name, so you know what is coming from where for implementation on that end.

The only concern I do have, just so that y'all are aware, is there is a real distinct possibility that some of these smaller sites have no social media at all developed, so that would mean that it would have to be created to initiate that.
And then Brandon, we can help you with the Google side, since I've got access to AssetVinus.
Yeah, that'll work.
And if it's… if it's properties that need social profiles created that we don't own, that's… We may….
Dr.
Yeah, honestly, we may… those properties may not get activated on social, with as large as asset is.
We're only allowed to create Meta doesn't like us, we can only create sometimes one property per day, per person, if we're lucky.
So, some properties, if we don't… if we have to create them, we may just not be able to do the social component at first.
Okay.
Any idea that's there, Morgan or Patrick?
I think we just need to know, like.
how many this applies to?
Like, is it 40 of the 60?
Is it all of that?
Is it closer to 60?
Is this less than that?
And then we can just work from there.
Yeah, we can find out probably in a day or two.
I'll message….
Let us know, and then we can… we can make a call, then, on what we want to do.
Nope.
Yeah, that'll work.

Well, once we do get the feeds, come in, sometimes it takes, like, a day from the time that you turn it on to our team receiving it, but usually we, after we have the feed, and the Google access set up, we can usually get all features live within about 2 business days.
So, we'll keep you in the loop as we get those feeds coming through, and then we'll also update you, every profile we get done.
We'll let you know as soon as these all get live.
The last piece of onboarding, would be the brand guidelines.
So, really everything that we need to get this live, as you've seen before, Brandon, is those two steps, and then we can get all of the features live.
The brand guidelines is an additional step if you want to customize your brand voice at a property level.
And then we also have the ability to intake SEO keywords.
So, what we would need there is a list of SEO keywords from you and your team, and then we get those SEO keywords entered throughout the posts.
Those are both optional, so those are optional steps if you want to customize your brand voice, but not necessary if you didn't want to.
I'll be honest, it's unlikely that we even have brand guidelines for the majority of this grouping.
Once again, if they weren't able to afford digital and all the things, it's very unlikely that we've got more than a logo to throw your way, and what's on the website can be scrubbed.

If there are a couple, though, I know that we have a couple that are running SEO, and we have a couple that do have full brand guidelines, maybe we can notate that, Brandon, in our, like, tracker thing of which ones had what to see if maybe that improves performance, over the course of the pilot, but I'll just… Probably tell you it's going to be likely less than about 5, that will have that third step.
Yeah.
And that's totally fine, too.
We do have, like, a brand guide document that we can send over.
Maybe that's something we look at on the next check-in call, or even maybe the month after.
Let's focus on getting these live first, and then we can look at how to further optimize them after that.
Sounds like maybe that's the best route.
That sounds good, because I think I know of a few, and we can notate it, but it'll be a lot harder to track that down, because I would just say it's very unlikely.
Most of these got a little logo that we made, and then.
off it goes.
Yeah.
Yeah, that sounds good.
The brand guidelines for the Google tool is a little bit more like brand voice, where we're intaking terminology that you want to use, or maybe want to hold off on.
It's more like, do we use the term apartment homes, or units, or apartments, and do we include the word luxury, or is it luxe?
It's getting a little bit more granular with the brand voice, more so than, like, the brand guideline with logos.
So, but those are things that we can talk about after these get live.
And then, what to expect post-kickoff?
We touched on this already, but that would be the ongoing check-ins.

So, we'll shoot for monthly in the beginning, I'll send over a recap after today, we'll work on implementation now, and then after we get these live, I'll reach out to schedule that first one, and then we'll just make that, like, reoccurring every month for the beginning.
And then I included these in here, just because I'll be sending this deck over, so you have all resources.
Morgan and I are going to be your point of contact, so, as you need things, you can reach out to either or both of us, but you also will have the support website.
Or, not website, email, and then you have the, billing email as well.
So, if you wanted to reach out directly to them, you can.
Otherwise, if you reach out to us and get us questions, we'll route it to the right place.

And then I wanted to include this, because I'll be sending this over, but wanted to put together some best practices that we've seen, with the customers that we've worked with, and things like making sure your concessions are accurate, because we'll be pulling concessions into your profile.
A little information about the best quality photos and how it looks on Google.
And then we talked about the SEO visibility, and then brand consistency as well, but wanted to make sure that you have this when I send it over.
That's great, thank you.
Alright, any questions from anyone so far?
Hopefully at one of the easier meetings of your day.
Hopefully.
Yeah, no.
nothing.
This has been less painful than the others, and I was already excited about getting this launched, so, like, no problem here.
Okay, two things you said, Christy, are what we aim for.
One, the listing looks so cool.
Yes, that's one of our goals, because… We want to get as much information about the property on there as possible, and then as pain-free as possible.
So you hit on two things.
That's what we try to accomplish with our partners.
Sounds like we hit it.
Yeah, no, so far, I'm happy with what I've seen on the two.

Obviously, it'll take some time to see how the product really drives additional traffic, which is the benefit of the, … little spreadsheet that Brandon put together, which I don't know if you guys had discussed that yet.
No, we haven't.
Nope.
So it's likely gonna be what we review every month, anyway.
… But we utilize Pilot Success Workbooks at Asset for a variety of things.
… One of them being, obviously, this.
So, we will…

Obviously, launch… when we launch, asset team is going to take a look at between social posts, engagements, and in GA for analytics to determine… and Q&As, or posting special offers, to determine Launch month is… September, let's say, officially.
We'll look at… we're gonna count posts, organic web traffic, … on social and in Google to determine how much did each property post?
How engaged were they?
What was the organic web traffic in Google Analytics for those 3 months pre-launch?
We'll do the same thing with you guys post-launch, and we'll determine by formula, the average change month over month, looking at only 3 launch months and launch months.
I'm gonna… generally, I have the… setup, I don't include a launch month in… changes in the delta between pre and post.
I don't know, let's say for some reason, a product launches mid-month, or, you know, it has an error and we have to delay by a week, or what have you.
There's any number of reasons why launch month should not be included in what I'm looking at changes.
So this is what we're going to be filling in, property by property, over the next 90 days.
because we need to not just say, oh, we had a great portfolio-wide performance for third-party management.

I have to be able to go to every single individual owner and convince them that they you know, this was a successful use of your time and everything, because every single one of these properties on this list… That's a different owner.
Every one of them.
So….

We can help too, Brandon, like, we also track in reporting just… views, clicks, calls, requests for directions on Google, so we have our own reporting, too, that we… it pulls from your Google Analytics, so we can also help there, too.
We're not strangers to our portfolios like yours.
A lot of our customers have to report back to client ownership groups, excuse me.
And so we are happy to provide any kind of analytics over time.
It does take, I think you're right here, 3 months, 6, 9, 12 months.
It does take time, because it's an organic tool, not a paid ads, but we also have Reporting as well that we can provide you to help out.
Yeah, that'd be great, because, you know, the second thing to this is, you know, I wanted to look at postings.
I wanted to look at engagement on the posts, or what have you, whatever's going on.
But the follow-up to this will be a 6-month check-in, and then a 9-month check-in, as opposed to, I'm gonna track it every single month.
I know that this is, like, an exercise, and We're gonna hate ourselves for putting this together every month to see a 2% increase.
And I know that in 6 months' time, we're gonna see a 12-15% increase, a 20% increase.
Yeah.
to organic web traffic and activity organically.

I know that, honestly, on a majority of these properties, these first four tabs, even these first 6 tabs, are likely gonna see 20-50% increases in performance, just because some properties don't post on social media, don't post on… Some properties are gonna see 100% performance improvement.
I'm totally expecting that.
… So, I, I, I fully expect, you know, these first 4 tabs, I'm… ideally, I'd like to see at least a 25% performance improvement over pre-launch.
Because that's just about what I think is gonna happen on average across all these properties, is about a 25% improvement.
Organic web traffic, in the end of the pilot, I'm expecting a 2-5% increase in web traffic.
as, like, my defined goal.
I know it's not gonna be huge, it's organic, it takes forever, but….
That's.
I think in 90 days, 2-5% increase organic web traffic is probably doable, because we're finally adding in more social media content and more organic Google content.
Yeah, for sure.
Does that sound about it?
In the ballpark of what y'all normally.
I think so.
I think so.
I mean, you said it's a long… it's a long play.

I mean, we have seen more than that year over year from our partners, who go from no updating and no posting to adding this Automation on top, so it's been upwards of 20-30%, but I think starting with the expectation of 5-10, I think, makes sense.
And then for here, number of posts, you're gonna get… at least 8 a month from us, so I don't know what you're getting currently.
Manual.
No.
Zero on a lot.
Yeah, so you should… you'll see a big lift there.

Of course, we have to work out which ones have social and which don't, but, this is designed to be… 100% consistent, so you know exactly what you're getting every day, every week, every month from a tool like this on these social channels.
Perfect.
And that's… that's going to be the consistency we want to see.
If I know I've got a property that's not doing it, months 1 plus 1 plus 2 plus 3 should be the exact same every single month, because I know.
Exactly, yeah.
Yeah, we do two a week, so on average, Morgan and Kylie love this.
It's like 8.
66 posts per month if you take two per week, so it's… some months it's 9, sometimes it's 8, but you're gonna get that every single month.
Yeah, perfect.
Yeah, and it's basically the same thing all the way through, just looking at organic traffic, or… Everything.
So, it's all about the same.
Nice.
Is there anything out of that that you want me to add into a success workbook that I may have missed?
Will you pull it back up?
I mean, I… some of our customers track… this is tougher, but track, like, hours saved and time savings.
Oh, I'm… I can't go into that.
I already know that half these properties don't post.
Exactly, so that's what I was gonna say, so it might not be necessary here, but I think you're on the right track here.
Hey, let's compare what we're getting against what we had in the past.
I think probably our number one indicator of success for partners to roll us out.
For owner-operator, it's like time savings.
They're doing it internally, and that's man hours that they could allocate elsewhere.
